text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Okonkwo, Renata
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED] (Age: 39)
SEX: Female
ORDERING PROVIDER: [Breast Oncology / Radiology]
PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist]
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: Fresh in formalin
CLINICAL HISTORY: 39-year-old premenopausal female, Nigerian-American, presenting with a self-discovered left breast mass at 10 o'clock position. No prior history of breast disease. Significant family history of breast cancer (mother diagnosed at age 42) and ovarian cancer (maternal aunt). Ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy performed for pathologic diagnosis.

---

SPECIMEN IDENTIFIER
Case Number: [SP-XXXXXX]
Specimen Type: Core needle biopsy, left breast, 10 o'clock position
Procedure: Ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy
Number of Cores Submitted: 4
Needle Gauge: 14-gauge
Clinical Indication: Irregular hypoechoic mass with angular margins, BI-RADS 5, left breast upper outer quadrant

---

DATE OF PROCEDURE: [Date to be added programmatically]
DATE OF RECEIPT IN PATHOLOGY: [Date to be added programmatically]

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION

Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name, MRN, and "left breast core biopsy," are four cylindrical cores of tan-white to pale-gray fibrofatty tissue. The cores measure approximately 1.4 to 1.6 cm in length and 0.1 to 0.2 cm in greatest diameter each (aggregate tissue length approximately 5.8 cm). The cores have a firm consistency with focal areas of chalky-white, firm tissue interspersed with adipose tissue. No grossly hemorrhagic or cystic areas are identified. All four cores are submitted entirely, wrapped in lens paper, and submitted in one cassette, labeled A1.

Representative sections submitted:
- A1: All four cores, entirely submitted

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION

All four cores are examined. The histologic sections demonstrate cores of breast parenchyma extensively replaced by a highly cellular malignant epithelial neoplasm. The tumor cells are arranged predominantly in solid sheets and confluent nests with focal trabecular architecture. The neoplasm is characterized by marked nuclear pleomorphism: the nuclei are large, vesicular, and highly irregular in shape and size, with coarse chromatin and multiple prominent nucleoli (nuclear grade 3). The cytoplasm is moderate in amount and eosinophilic. Mitotic figures are abundant, including atypical mitoses, numbering greater than 20 per 10 high-power fields (40x objective). Geographic central necrosis ("comedo-type" necrosis) is present within the solid tumor nests. Extensive chronic and acute inflammatory infiltrate is noted at the periphery of tumor nests, including a notable tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) population estimated at approximately 30-40% stromal TILs.

The tumor demonstrates pushing borders with focal infiltrative growth into the surrounding fibrous stroma. The adjacent non-neoplastic breast parenchyma, where identifiable, shows normal lobular architecture. No ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) is definitively identified in the cores, though the sampling is limited by the nature of core biopsy. No lymphovascular invasion is identified in the submitted cores, though evaluation is limited by the biopsy nature of the specimen.

The overall histologic appearance is that of invasive carcinoma of no special type (NST; formerly invasive ductal carcinoma), high grade. Tubule formation is minimal/absent (score 3). Nuclear pleomorphism is marked (score 3). Mitotic rate is high (score 3). Nottingham combined histologic grade: 9/9, Grade 3.

Tumor Infiltrating Lymphocytes (TILs): Approximately 30-40% stromal TILs (moderate). Assessment performed per International TIL Working Group guidelines.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on block A1):

1. Estrogen Receptor (ER) [Clone SP1, Ventana]: NEGATIVE. 0% of tumor nuclei staining. Allred score: 0/8. Interpretation: Negative per ASCO/CAP 2020 guidelines (threshold <1%).

2. Progesterone Receptor (PR) [Clone 1E2, Ventana]: NEGATIVE. 0% of tumor nuclei staining. Allred score: 0/8. Interpretation: Negative per ASCO/CAP 2020 guidelines.

3. HER2 [Clone 4B5, Ventana]: 1+ (INCOMPLETE membrane staining in >10% of tumor cells). Interpretation: NEGATIVE per ASCO/CAP 2018 guidelines. FISH NOT PERFORMED (IHC 1+ does not meet criteria for reflex FISH per current guidelines).

4. Ki-67 [Clone MIB-1]: 85% of tumor nuclei positive. Interpretation: High proliferation index.

5. Cytokeratin 5/6 [Clone D5/16B4]: POSITIVE. Diffuse cytoplasmic and membranous staining in approximately 60% of tumor cells. Interpretation: Positive, consistent with basal-like immunophenotype.

6. EGFR [Clone 3C6, Ventana]: POSITIVE, 2+ (moderate complete membranous staining in >10% of tumor cells). Interpretation: Positive, further supporting basal-like phenotype.

7. p53 [Clone DO-7]: ABERRANT STAINING PATTERN. Diffuse, strong nuclear positivity in approximately 80% of tumor cells. Interpretation: Aberrant/overexpression pattern, consistent with a TP53 gain-of-function missense mutation (as opposed to null pattern). Correlation with molecular testing recommended.

8. E-cadherin [Clone 36B5]: POSITIVE (retained, diffuse membranous staining). Interpretation: Supports carcinoma of ductal/no-special-type lineage; lobular carcinoma excluded.

ADDITIONAL STUDIES (PENDING):
- Germline BRCA1/BRCA2 testing: PENDING (patient referred for hereditary cancer genetic counseling given family history of breast and ovarian cancer; blood-based germline panel ordered)
- Next-generation sequencing (comprehensive somatic tumor profiling): PENDING per oncology request

---

SYNOPTIC SUMMARY (CAP Protocol)

Specimen Type: Core needle biopsy
Site: Left breast, upper outer quadrant, 10 o'clock, 4 cm from nipple
Histologic Type: Invasive carcinoma of no special type (NST) / Invasive ductal carcinoma
Histologic Grade: Grade 3 (Nottingham score 9/9)
  - Tubule formation: 3 (minimal/absent)
  - Nuclear pleomorphism: 3 (marked)
  - Mitotic count: 3 (>20/10 HPF)
Tumor Size (histologic): Cannot be determined on core biopsy; correlate with imaging (3.2 cm by ultrasound)
Lymphovascular Invasion: Not identified (limited by biopsy)
DCIS Component: Not identified in cores
Estrogen Receptor: Negative (0%, Allred 0)
Progesterone Receptor: Negative (0%, Allred 0)
HER2: Negative (IHC 1+)
Ki-67: 85%
Molecular Subtype (clinical surrogate): Triple-negative / Basal-like
Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocytes: Approximately 30-40% stromal TILs

---

PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS

LEFT BREAST, UPPER OUTER QUADRANT, ULTRASOUND-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY (4 CORES, 14-GAUGE):

INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA, NO SPECIAL TYPE (NST), GRADE 3 (NOTTINGHAM 9/9)
- TRIPLE-NEGATIVE (ER NEGATIVE / PR NEGATIVE / HER2 NEGATIVE)
- BASAL-LIKE IMMUNOPHENOTYPE (CK5/6 POSITIVE, EGFR POSITIVE)
- Ki-67 PROLIFERATION INDEX: 85% (HIGH)
- ABERRANT p53 EXPRESSION: PRESENT (consistent with TP53 gain-of-function mutation)
- TUMOR-INFILTRATING LYMPHOCYTES: MODERATE (~30-40% STROMAL TILs)
- LYMPHOVASCULAR INVASION: NOT IDENTIFIED IN CORES SUBMITTED

NOTE: Germline BRCA1/BRCA2 testing and comprehensive somatic tumor NGS profiling are pending at time of this report. Results will be reported as addendum upon availability. Correlation with clinical findings, imaging, and multidisciplinary tumor board discussion is recommended.

---

Signed: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
[Institution Name]

Electronic signature applied. Report finalized.

Addendum pending: Germline genetic panel results; somatic NGS tumor profiling results.
